sutta: 8 now a certain brahmin visited the exalted 1 greeted him and after changing after exchanging the usual polite park sat down at 1 side so seated he said to the exalted 1 this master gotama is my a vowel this is my view there is no self-agency atakarro no other agency and the buddha said never brahmin have i seen or heard of such an avow such a view pray how can 1 step onwards how can 1 step back yet say there is no self-agency there is no other agency what thank you brahman is there such a thing as initiative yes sir that being so a man known to initiate yes sir well brahmin since there is initiative and men are known to initiate this is among men the self-agency this is the other agency what thank you brahman is there such a thing as stepping away stepping forth for halting standing and stepping towards anything yes sir that being so are men known to do all these things yes sir well brahman since there are such things as stepping away stepping forth and the rest and men are known to do these things this is among men the self-agency this is the other agency never brahmin have i seen or heard of such an avow such a view as yours pray how can 1 step onwards step back and say there is no self-agency there is no other agency and the brahmin said this indeed is wonderful master gautama etc and henceforth to live ends i will go to master of gotama for help that's the end of the sutal so at the end the brahmana took refuge with the buddha this suit is quite interesting 1 because here this man is saying there is no atakaro ata is the self karo is the 1 who does so here they translate it as no self agency a no no self-agency no self who does this is something like saying there is no self it's man this man is saying there's such there's no such thing as a self but the buddha in our buddhist teaching although the buddha said the thought the doctrine of anata which is normally translated as no self actually that should probably be translated as no soul because soul is supposed to be something that is permanent unchanging that exists forever but in the buddha's teaching there is a self but it is an impermanent self it is an impermanent cell that is why the buddha said now there is such a thing as initiative and men are known to use their initiative stepping forward and stepping backwards etc so the buddha is saying here that there is such a thing as a self probably what he means is it is an impermanent self so this um suta in a way shows that this theory of no self was probably not started by the buddha probably during the buddha's time there was already such views as there is no self and so it's just that the buddha's interpretation is that there is no soul such a thing such soul meaning such a thing that is permanent unchanging
